perturbation.original_text: For a period of one year subsequent to the termination of discussions regarding a Transaction, neither Party shall, without the prior written consent of the other Party, directly or indirectly solicit for hire any person currently employed by the other Party (or any of its subsidiaries) with whom the hiring Party first has contact, or who first becomes known to the hiring Party. In the course of the Parties' discussions and due diligence with respect to the proposed Transaction; provided, however, that the foregoing provision shall not prevent either Party, without such consent, from employing any employee who (i) contacts the hiring Party directly at his or her own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from the hiring Party, (ii) responds to a mass media solicitation or advertisement consistent with the hiring Party's past practices that is not directed at employees of the other Party or (iii) is identified by a third party executive search firm or employment agency without any assistance from such Party.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of one year subsequent to the termination of discussions regarding a Transaction, neither Party shall directly or indirectly solicit for hire any person currently employed by the other Party (or any of its subsidiaries) with whom the hiring Party first has contact, or who first becomes known to the hiring Party. In the course of the Parties' discussions and due diligence with respect to the proposed Transaction; provided, however, that the foregoing provision shall not prevent either Party, without such consent, from employing any employee who (i) contacts the hiring Party directly at his or her own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from the hiring Party, (ii) responds to a mass media solicitation or advertisement consistent with the hiring Party's past practices that is not directed at employees of the other Party or (iii) is identified by a third party executive search firm or employment agency without any assistance from such Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "without the prior written consent of the other Party", it creates a contradiction. It states that neither party shall solicit for hire, but then it also says they can employ certain employees. The omitted phrase allowed for an exception with consent. Now it implies the solicitation is fully prohibited. This inconsistency creates doubt for enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Solicitation. (a) For a period of one year subsequent to the termination of discussions regarding a Transaction, neither Party shall directly or indirectly solicit for hire any person currently employed by the other Party (or any of its subsidiaries) with whom the hiring Party first has contact, or who first becomes known to the hiring Party. In the course of the Parties' discussions and due diligence with respect to the proposed Transaction; provided, however, that the foregoing provision shall not prevent either Party, without such consent, from employing any employee who (i) contacts the hiring Party directly at his or her own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from the hiring Party, (ii) responds to a mass media solicitation or advertisement consistent with the hiring Party's past practices that is not directed at employees of the other Party or (iii) is identified by a third party executive search firm or employment agency without any assistance from such Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that neither party shall solicit for hire without the prior written consent of the other party, but allows employment under certain conditions. The changed text removes the 'without the prior written consent of the other Party' phrase, creating a contradiction. The modified text now implies a complete prohibition of solicitation while still allowing employment under specific conditions. This contradicts the 'No Solicitation' clause, as now the ability to 'employ' under the exceptions could be interpreted as a solicitation violation, leading to ambiguity and a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: At any time after termination of discussions by either Party with respect to the Transaction, upon the request of the Furnishing Party, the Receiving Party shall promptly (and in no event later than five business days after such request) (a) redeliver or cause to be redelivered to the Furnishing Party all copies of all Primary Evaluation Material in the possession or control of the Receiving Party or its Representatives which is in a visual or written format and erase or destroy all copies of all such Primary Evaluation Material which is stored in electronic format, and (b) destroy or cause to be destroyed all Derivative Evaluation Material in the possession or control of the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: At any time after termination of discussions by either Party with respect to the Transaction, upon the request of the Furnishing Party, the Receiving Party shall promptly (and in no event later than five business days after such request) (a) redeliver or cause to be redelivered to the Furnishing Party all copies of all Primary Evaluation Material in the possession or control of the Receiving Party or its Representatives which is in a visual or written format and erase or destroy all copies of all such Primary Evaluation Material which is stored in electronic format.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the clause "(b) destroy or cause to be destroyed all Derivative Evaluation Material in the possession or control of the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives.", it contradicts the later statement: "Notwithstanding the return, destruction or erasure of Evaluation Material hereunder, the Receiving Party and its Representatives shall continue to be bound by their confidentiality and other obligations hereunder." as it references the erasure of all Evaluation Material, which is no longer fully applicable since derivative material may not be destroyed according to the edited section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return and Destruction of Evaluation Material. At any time after termination of discussions by either Party with respect to the Transaction, upon the request of the Furnishing Party, the Receiving Party shall promptly (and in no event later than five business days after such request) (a) redeliver or cause to be redelivered to the Furnishing Party all copies of all Primary Evaluation Material in the possession or control of the Receiving Party or its Representatives which is in a visual or written format and erase or destroy all copies of all such Primary Evaluation Material which is stored in electronic format. Nothing herein shall obligate the Receiving Party to provide any Derivative Evaluation Material to the Furnishing Party. Notwithstanding the return, destruction or erasure of Evaluation Material hereunder, the Receiving Party and its Representatives shall continue to be bound by their confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the destruction of Derivative Evaluation Material, while the changed text omits this requirement. The contradicted text references the "return, destruction or erasure of Evaluation Material", which is now partially untrue as derivative material may not be destroyed according to the edited section. This creates a contradiction because the Receiving Party's obligations regarding derivative material are no longer consistent throughout the document, making the confidentiality clause ambiguous.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of any provision of this Agreement by it or any of its Representatives, and that in addition to all other remedies which either Party may have, each Party shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity. No failure or delay by either Party in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right, power or privilege hereunder. In the event that either Party should institute proceedings to enforce any provision of this Agreement, the prevailing Party in such proceedings shall be entitled to recover all expenses relating to the enforcement of this Agreement, including reasonable attorneys' fees and costs, in addition to any other remedies.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of any provision of this Agreement by it or any of its Representatives, and that in addition to all other remedies which either Party may have, each Party shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity. No failure or delay by either Party in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right, power or privilege hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "In the event that either Party should institute proceedings to enforce any provision of this Agreement, the prevailing Party in such proceedings shall be entitled to recover all expenses relating to the enforcement of this Agreement, including reasonable attorneys' fees and costs, in addition to any other remedies.", it contradicts the 'remedies' available and normally enforceable. While other remedies exist, the removal of the attorney's fees clause would impact the cost incurred by the party initiating enforcement. This creates doubt in regards to the actual cost enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies. Each Party agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of any provision of this Agreement by it or any of its Representatives, and that in addition to all other remedies which either Party may have, each Party shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity. No failure or delay by either Party in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right, power or privilege hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes a clause about the prevailing party in enforcement proceedings being entitled to recover expenses, including attorney's fees. The changed text omits this clause. The contradicted text is essentially a copy of the original language. By removing the attorney's fees clause in the Changed Text from the Original Text, there is an in-text contradiction because the remedy available in the Contradicted Text is no longer as comprehensive as it once was due to this key omission, impacting the cost of enforcement and thus contradicting the scope of remedies available.

perturbation.original_text: The terms of this Agreement shall not be construed to limit either Party's right to independently develop or acquire products without use of the other Party's Evaluation Material. The Furnishing Party acknowledges that the Receiving Party may currently or in the future develop information internally, or receive information from other parties, that is similar to the Evaluation Material. Accordingly, nothing in this Agreement will be construed as a representation or agreement that the Receiving Party will not develop or have developed for its products, concepts, systems, or techniques that are similar to or compete with the products, concepts, systems or techniques contemplated by or embodied in the Evaluation Material, provided that the Receiving Party does not violate any of its obligations under this Agreement in connection with such development.
perturbation.changed_text: The terms of this Agreement shall not be construed to limit either Party's right to independently develop or acquire products without use of the other Party's Evaluation Material. The Furnishing Party acknowledges that the Receiving Party may currently or in the future develop information internally, or receive information from other parties, that is similar to the Evaluation Material. Accordingly, nothing in this Agreement will be construed as a representation or agreement that the Receiving Party will not develop or have developed for its products, concepts, systems, or techniques that are similar to or compete with the products, concepts, systems or techniques contemplated by or embodied in the Evaluation Material.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "provided that the Receiving Party does not violate any of its obligations under this Agreement in connection with such development.", it alters the constraints on the Receiving Party. The original clause clarified that the Receiving Party's independent development was permissible only if they didn't violate any other terms of the agreement. By removing this condition, the agreement could be interpreted as allowing the Receiving Party to use confidential information obtained through Evaluation Material in their independent development, contradicting the core principle of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidentiality and Use of Evaluation Material. (a) Confidentiality of Evaluation Material. All Evaluation Material (i) shall be used solely for the purpose of evaluating and considering the Transaction; (ii) shall be kept strictly confidential by the Receiving Party; and (iii) shall be provided by the Receiving Party solely to those of its Representatives to whom disclosure is reasonably required to facilitate the Receiving Party's evaluation or consideration of the Transaction, it being the intention of the Parties to restrict the dissemination of Evaluation Material to as small a working group as practicable. The Parties agree that all Evaluation Material is and shall remain the property of the Furnishing Party. Before providing access to Evaluation Material to any Representative, the Receiving Party shall inform such Representative of the confidentiality of the Evaluation Material, and shall advise such Representative that, by accepting possession of or access to such information, such Representative is agreeing to be bound by this Agreement. Each Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the Receiving Party to independently develop products similar to the Evaluation Material, but only if they did not violate other terms of the agreement. The changed text removes this condition. The contradicted text mandates that all Evaluation Material be kept strictly confidential and used solely for evaluating the transaction. By removing the condition, the Receiving Party could use confidential information (Evaluation Material) in their independent development, directly contradicting the confidentiality clause and thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party consents and submits to the exclusive jurisdiction of the federal and state courts in the State of Georgia, and the city of Atlanta, for the adjudication of any action, suit, or proceeding arising out of or otherwise relating to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party consents and submits to the jurisdiction of the federal and state courts in the State of Georgia, and the city of Atlanta, for the adjudication of any action, suit, or proceeding arising out of or otherwise relating to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word "exclusive", it no longer limits the case/issue only to those courts, therefore, the case can also be opened in another court outside of the State of Georgia. By removing the word it allows another judge from another location to judge on the same case, which is a contradiction on the statement before it, but it also makes it contradictory. By allowing a new judge, it can also make the case open again after the prior case was closed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Governing Law; Forum. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the internal laws of the State of Georgia, without regard to the principles of the conflict of laws thereof. Each Party consents and submits to the jurisdiction of the federal and state courts in the State of Georgia, and the city of Atlanta, for the adjudication of any action, suit, or proceeding arising out of or otherwise relating to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the courts in Georgia have 'exclusive jurisdiction.' The changed text removes 'exclusive,' meaning other courts could also have jurisdiction. The contradicted text reiterates the initial exclusive jurisdiction. The removal of 'exclusive' directly contradicts the clause asserting exclusive jurisdiction, creating a strong in-text contradiction as the agreement now implies multiple jurisdictions are valid for adjudicating disputes, where only one was specified before. This adheres to the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' as essential language was removed that created a contradiction.
